title: Sigmoid Linear Unit
description: A smooth activation function that scales each value by its own sigmoid score.
openingSummary: The sigmoid linear unit (SiLU) smoothly scales each value by its own sigmoid score, making it a softer alternative to ReLU and an important stepping stone to gated designs such as SwiGLU.

whatItIs.title: What It Is
whatItIs.body: The sigmoid linear unit (SiLU) is an activation function that multiplies each value x by sigmoid(x). Large positive values pass through strongly, values near zero are softened, and negative values are reduced smoothly instead of being cut off sharply. In transformer feed-forward layers, that gives the model a softer filter than ReLU.

whyItExists.title: Why It Exists
whyItExists.body: SiLU optimizes for a smoother activation rule. Instead of a hard cutoff, it lets the negative side fade gradually, which often fits modern wide feed-forward layers better than a sharp zero clamp.

howItWorks.title: How It Works
howItWorks.body: SiLU works one value at a time by multiplying that value by its own sigmoid score. Large positive values mostly survive, small values are softened, and negative values shrink rather than snapping to zero. In a transformer feed-forward layer, the model applies that rule after expanding into a wider hidden state and before projecting back down.

mathOrComputeSchema.title: Math Or Compute Schema
mathOrComputeSchema.body: The formula below shows the smooth self-gating rule directly. The sigmoid term is what turns one value into its own soft gate.

comparedToNearbyModules.title: Compared To Nearby Modules
comparedToNearbyModules.body: Compared with ReLU and LeakyReLU, SiLU is the smooth option in this activation family. It still stays inside one dense feed-forward path, but it prepares the reader for the stronger two-branch gate used by SwiGLU.

exampleArchitectures.title: Example Architectures
exampleArchitectures.body: Many recent decoder-only language models and gated feed-forward blocks use SiLU or a close relative, especially when authors want a smoother dense baseline before moving to SwiGLU.

limitationsAndTradeoffs.title: Limitations And Tradeoffs
limitationsAndTradeoffs.body: SiLU is smoother than ReLU-family activations, but it is still a dense per-token activation, not a routing or expert mechanism. Every token still runs through the full shared feed-forward path.

whyItStillMatters.title: Why It Still Matters
whyItStillMatters.body: SiLU sits at an important bridge point between classic activation pages and modern gated feed-forward pages. Readers who understand SiLU can usually decode SwiGLU much faster.

computeFlow.alt: Activation comparison chart showing how rectified linear unit, leaky rectified linear unit, and sigmoid linear unit reshape positive and negative values

relu.negativeBranch: Clamp every negative hidden value to 0
relu.mainTradeoff: Simple and sparse, but fully drops negative signal

leakyRelu.negativeBranch: Keep a small slope alpha x for x < 0
leakyRelu.transitionShape: Hard corner with a weak negative path
leakyRelu.mainTradeoff: Keeps some negative gradient, but still uses a piecewise-linear cutoff

silu.negativeBranch: Shrink negative values smoothly with x sigma(x)
silu.mainTradeoff: Smoother feature filtering, but more complex than the ReLU family baseline

x.definition: One input value before the activation rule is applied.

sigma.definition: Sigmoid function applied to that same input value.

negativeBehavior.label: Negative values shrink smoothly instead of snapping to 0
